subject: [PLATFORM] Generate Cert-Service certs with Cert-Manager
commit_message:
[PLATFORM] Generate Cert-Service certs with Cert-Manager

Utilize Cert-Manager to secure communication between
Cert-Service and its clients, adjust templates and
configs.

Issue-ID: OOM-2712

Change-Id: I96426b1a184b4d254575e76d29214d9deda08cce
Signed-off-by: Remigiusz Janeczek <remigiusz.janeczek@nokia.com>

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

> 
> Just cert-manager is required. And cert manager is one of ONAP requirements.
> Certificates are generated by Self-signed issuer (provided by certmanager). 
> So cmpv2 has no impact.

@Sylvain - our overall plan is, in Istanbul release, to deprecate CertService client usage as init container and focus only on CMPv2 issuer, where we are planning to implement proper cert update scenario. 

So you are right, starting Istanbul release, if you want to enroll certificates from CMPv2 servers you need Cert-Manager. And yes, without Cert-Manager, CMPv2 CertService won't come up, due to missing secret with cert which should be created by Cert-Manager.
